segments.speaker: JACINTA
segments.en: Yes, we do our best to organise it that way, and we usually manage it.
segments.zh: 是的，我们尽力这样安排，而且通常都能做到。

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.en: Good.
segments.zh: 好的，那没问题。那么，我想继续……

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.en: That's fine.

segments.speaker: CLIENT
segments.en: Right, so I'd like to go ahead and ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As many of you here today have worked in the hotel industry for some time, I'm sure you have experienced the problem of high staff turnover in your hotels.
segments.zh: 在座各位很多都在酒店行业工作了一段时间，我相信你们一定遇到过酒店员工高流动率的问题。每家酒店都依赖忠诚且有经验的员工来确保一切顺利运转。如果员工不断更换，会让每个人的工作变得困难。但为什么很多酒店员工频繁离职呢？当然，很多酒店工作技能要求低、薪水不高。许多经理认为这是高流动率的主要原因——但他们没有意识到，缺乏培训也是一个巨大因素。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Every hotel relies on having loyal and experienced members of staff who make sure that everything runs smoothly.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If staff are constantly changing, it can make life difficult for everyone.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But why do staff leave frequently in many hotels?

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Of course, many hotel jobs, such as low-skilled and are not well-paid.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A lot of managers think it's this and the long hours that are the main causes of high staff turnover – but what they don't realise is that it's the lack of training in many hotel jobs which is a huge factor.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, what kind of problems does a high turnover of staff cause?
segments.zh: 那么，高员工流动率会带来哪些问题呢？嗯，不断招聘新员工非常耗时，经理们可能不得不在等待新员工到岗期间承担一些职责。这意味着他们没有时间考虑不那么紧迫的问题，比如如何改进服务。员工离职也会严重影响他们留下的同事。这会对留下的员工产生负面影响，他们可能开始觉得自己也应该考虑离开。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Well, having to recruit new staff all the time can be very time-consuming, and managers may have to cover some duties while waiting for new staff to arrive.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This means they don't have time to think about less immediate problems such as how to improve their service.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When staff leave, it can also severely affect the colleagues they leave behind.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It has a negative effect on remaining staff, who may start to feel that they too should be thinking about leaving.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So, what can be done to change this situation?
segments.zh: 那么，如何改变这种状况呢？首先，经理们应该停止犯那些让员工感到沮丧和怨恨的基本错误。例如，在安排班次时，确保不要给某些员工特殊待遇。所有员工都应该对工作时间有一定的选择权，而且每个人都应该上一些晚班和周末班。如果你公平对待员工，他们就更有可能在需要额外人手时挺身而出。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Firstly, managers should stop making basic errors which leave their staff feeling upset and resentful.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When organising shifts, for example, make sure you never give certain staff preferential treatment.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: All staff should be given some choice about when they work, and everyone should have to work some evening and weekend shifts.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If you treat staff fairly, they'll be more likely to step in and help when extra staff are needed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Keeping staff happy has other tangible benefits for the business.
segments.zh: 让员工开心还有其他实实在在的好处。以邓威奇酒店为例，它曾面临员工投诉的问题，为了解决这个问题，酒店投资于员工培训并改善了员工条件。不仅投诉水平下降了，他们还注意到每位顾客在住宿期间的平均消费显著增加。现在他们推出了一项客户忠诚度计划，进展非常顺利。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Take the Dunwich Hotel as an example.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It had been experiencing a problem with staff complaints and in order to deal with this, invested in staff training and improved staff conditions.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Not only did the level of complaints fall, but they also noticed a significant increase in the amount each customer spent during their stay.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They have now introduced a customer loyalty scheme which is going really well.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now I'd like to look at some ways you can reduce staff turnover in your hotels, and I'll do this by giving some examples of hotels where I've done some training recently.
segments.zh: 现在我想谈谈一些降低酒店员工流动率的方法，我会通过一些我最近培训过的酒店的例子来说明。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The Sun Club received feedback which showed that staff thought managers didn't value their opinions.
segments.zh: 太阳俱乐部收到的反馈显示，员工认为经理不重视他们的意见。他们没有感觉到自己是合作伙伴，为整个企业的成功做出贡献。这种情况已经改变。各级初级员工定期被邀请参加会议，他们的想法受到欢迎。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They weren't made to feel they were partners who were contributing to the success of the business as a whole.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This situation has changed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Junior staff at all levels are regularly invited to meetings where their ideas are welcomed.

items.prompt: Many hotel managers are unaware that their staff often leave because of

pairs.note: unaware 和 don't realise 都表示'没有意识到'
题干问员工离职的原因，原文指出是缺乏培训

paraphrase.explain: 定位到原文中'but what they don't realise is that it's the lack of training'，其中'don't realise'对应'unaware'，'lack of training'对应离职原因。

items.prompt: What is the impact of high staff turnover on managers?

pairs.p: problems does a high turnover of staff cause
pairs.note: 题干中的'impact'被替换为'problems...cause'，意思相同。

pairs.p: managers may have to cover some duties
pairs.note: 题干中的'managers'在原文中直接出现，但后面具体描述了影响。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'impact of high staff turnover'定位到原文'problems does a high turnover of staff cause'，然后找到对经理的具体影响：'managers may have to cover some duties'。

items.prompt: What mistake should managers always avoid?

pairs.note: mistake 和 errors 是同义替换，都表示错误。
avoid 和 stop making 都表示避免做某事。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问经理应避免什么错误，定位句说经理应停止犯基本错误，其中'stop making errors'对应'avoid mistake'。

items.prompt: What unexpected benefit did Dunwich Hotel notice after improving staff retention rates?

pairs.p: Not only did the level of complaints fall, but they also noticed a significant increase in the amount each customer spent during their stay
pairs.note: 题干问意外好处，原文用'not only...but also'结构引出两个结果，其中第二个是意外好处。

pairs.p: invested in staff training and improved staff conditions
pairs.note: 题干说提高员工留任率，原文具体描述为投资培训和改善条件，这是提高留任率的做法。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句先提到员工满意带来好处，然后以Dunwich Hotel为例，用'Not only...but also'结构引出两个结果，其中第二个'顾客消费增加'对应题干问的意外好处。

pairs.note: 题干中的专有名词在原文中完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'The Sun Club'在原文中逐字出现，直接定位到该俱乐部。
